And sometimes you actually script it in so you plant that, I've done this, you plant the objection in the person's head so they actually do bring it up so you can answer it. Again, this is not 101 selling, it's a little bit more advanced. Speaking of that, I'm going to ask you a question I didn't have written down, but I think it's an important one which could help everyone with not only handling objections and overcoming that, but with the whole sales process. How does psycho cybernetics fit into this whole thing? I mean, we can do a whole product on that, but I just want to touch upon it. Well, I mean, very simplistically, great salespeople have healthy self-images. So if you have a weak self-image, A, you are fearful of it being wounded because it's pretty weak to start with. And you are reluctant to be assertive because that invites possible confrontation. And you will be fearful of such things as objections because you will take it personally. So, the weakest salespeople in the world take every sort of response other than... Yes. Yes. Has a personal affront and rejection, right? And those people generally can't prosper in a selling environment. They're putting things on to the prospect that really aren't even there. The prospect isn't thinking about them at all. He's not reacting to them at all. He's reacting to price, ability to afford price that he does not really want to disclose. He's reacting to factors, not to the person. So, we did a Psycho-Sybernetics book. It's out of print, I think, but you'll probably find used copies of it on Amazon. Zero is the selling, right? Which is really all about the marriage of the inner game, not Norma Vitz appeal positive attitude stuff. But I mean, the inner game of how you see yourself and therefore how you see the selling environment and the prospect that you are interacting with. You know, one of the simplest techniques from Psycho-Sybernetics and Dr. Malz is the theater of the mind. I mean, they should be practicing their presentation mentally as well as overcoming the objections. Can't do it without a script. Right, valid, right. So, you can't go into the theater of your mind and play the movie of what's supposed to happen in the sales meeting we're about to have if I haven't scripted the sales meeting in the first place. Right, I haven't scripted the answers to the objections and all of that. But if you do that, man, that is so powerful. I mean, I do that before I speak. I go through, this is how it's gonna go, this is how it's gonna happen. So, you're addressing a bigger issue that is important to this whole program. And for anybody who doesn't yet own it, thinking about buying. See, most people get very poor results with selling and actually in their lives because they are very casual about all that they do. So, the person who, for example, takes an unscheduled incoming call from a prospect, he's not prepared in general to handle such a call at that moment in time. He's definitely not prepared for the specific prospect to whom he is going to speak. And so, he's gonna get not very good results. Better for, prepared for, planned and timed calls during a day than 40 unprepared for. So, this really is about taking a prepared approach, a non-casual, serious approach to every aspect of selling, including your own inner game that you apply to it. Absolutely, and we're gonna be talking about that on the bonus call that we do, which is coming up. We're gonna be going into the mental game of this as well. So, do you have something else to say? Yeah, you know, we were talking about that and we were talking a little bit about it at dinner. You said, when I first started and I had a right copy for my own business, I had to become the best copywriter possible. And I said, when I first started selling from one of my own businesses, I had to be the best salesperson out there because Chase didn't care how many visits I had or how many meetings I had. They wanted dollars in the bank to pay the mortgage. And so...
